---
title: "Welcome to the new Footy Forest"
description: "A fresh look for 2026 and a rebuilt prediction engine."
author: "Matt Nurse"
date: "2026-03-04"
categories: [news, model]
---

The Footy Forest has had a major overhaul for the 2026 season. Here's what's changed:

## New prediction engine

The model has been completely rebuilt using a stacked ensemble approach. Instead of relying on a single algorithm, predictions now come from a blend of five different ML models — random forest, SVM, GLMnet, naive Bayes, and a neural network — combined via penalised regression. The ensemble consistently achieves around 90% accuracy on the 2023–2025 validation set (ROC-AUC of 0.97).

## New predictor: venue effect

Previously the model used simple home ground advantage. Now, venue performance is estimated relative to ELO expectations using 5 years of historical data with Bayesian shrinkage. This captures the reality that some teams massively outperform at their home fortress (Port Adelaide at Adelaide Oval, Brisbane at the Gabba) while others have little or no home advantage at certain grounds.

## New site

The website has been rebuilt from scratch using Quarto. Cleaner, faster, and easier to maintain week to week. Predictions and power rankings update each round.

Looking forward to a great season. Go Dees.
